title: You
content:
You
what.

Participant
like, do you want to take

You
Exactly.

Participant
the stairs or the elevator? To the 50th floor. And I think taking the elevator is an easier way, and then we don't have to produce the medical records. We don't have to produce the doctors

You
.

Participant
who, you know, it sounds like your doctor, your therapist is, is probably not going to be beaten down the door to help us.

You
No.

Participant
And, you know, then all we have to prove is that you requested it, and you had a good faith belief that you were disabled, which, by the way, they also believe you had a good faith belief

You
Oh.

Participant
that you were able. Because, remember, they they granted you an accommodation. And so with just narrowing it down to the retaliation side of it, it's just simply that, you know, had you not have applied for, the the accommodations and filed the charges, they would have renewed the contract. And I think that's a much easier position to prove than, than, you know, the only effective accommodation for you is 100% work from home. And then we don't get drug into the weeds and the whole issue of, well, gee, you know, he had to go to these meetings that, that were essential functions of his job and, and he couldn't do that by working at home, which means he's not qualified because he can't, remember how they, they talked about those, um, Uh, I guess what, they, they changed their, their policy and required in person meetings and things like that, and their whole position is, well, we, he couldn't do, an essential function of the job is him going to these, you know, uh, team building things or these, uh, events from time to time, and because he can, you know, he's saying that he could only work 100% from home and that a 50% working in person and home, you know, that's not an effective
